sourceMovie.title: Totally Killer
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Totally Killer (2023) stuck because it commits to its horror premise without apologizing—viewers remember set pieces and momentum as much as plot. Nahnatchka Khan's control of tone keeps the ride coherent. The horror and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Trick 'r Treat
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Totally Killer clicked for you, Trick 'r Treat (2007) is a strong next stop: both trade in horror and comedy, with unease that lingers after the credits. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Totally Killer?
faq.answer: Try Trick 'r Treat, The Final Girls, Lisa Frankenstein—each shares DNA with Totally Killer while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Totally Killer?
faq.answer: Totally Killer (2023) is best described as Horror, Comedy, Science Fiction. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Totally Killer worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.8/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this horror lane.
